audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Judges / Shoftim national assembly, Benjamin confrontation, three battles, Gibeah ambush, and near-destruction of Benjamin narrative, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for all-Israel mobilization, Levite's self-protective testimony, absence of the murdered woman's voice, demand for perpetrators, Benjamin's collective refusal, troop figures, left-handed slingers, repeated YHWH inquiries, catastrophic Israelite losses, Phinehas and ark chronology, ambush tactics, Gibeah destruction, pursuit to Rimmon, massacre totals, killing across Benjaminite towns, animals, and city burning.

verses.restored: All children of Israel went out, and congregation gathered as one man, from Dan to Beersheba and land of Gilead, to YHWH at Mizpah.
Chiefs of all people, all tribes of Israel, presented themselves in assembly of people of Divine: four hundred thousand men on foot drawing sword.
Children of Benjamin heard that children of Israel had gone up to Mizpah. Children of Israel said, Speak: how did this evil happen?
The Levite, man of woman who had been murdered, answered and said, I came to Gibeah that belongs to Benjamin, I and my concubine, to lodge.
Masters of Gibeah rose against me and surrounded house against me by night. Me they intended to kill, and my concubine they raped/afflicted, and she died.
I took my concubine and cut her in pieces and sent her throughout every field of inheritance of Israel, because they committed a sexual outrage and disgrace in Israel.
Look, all of you are children of Israel. Give your word and counsel here.
All people arose as one man, saying, No man of us will go to his tent, and no man of us will turn aside to his house.
Now this is the thing we will do to Gibeah: we will go up against it by lot.

We will take ten men of a hundred from all tribes of Israel, and a hundred of a thousand, and a thousand of ten thousand, to take provisions for people, so that when they come to Gibeah of Benjamin they may do to it according to all outrage it has done in Israel.
Every man of Israel gathered against city as one man, joined together.
Tribes of Israel sent men through all tribe of Benjamin, saying, What is this evil that has happened among you?

Now give up the men, sons of beliyyaal/lawlessness, who are in Gibeah, so we may put them to death and remove evil from Israel. Children of Benjamin were not willing to listen to voice of their brothers, children of Israel.
Children of Benjamin gathered from cities to Gibeah to go out to battle with children of Israel.
Children of Benjamin were counted that day from cities: twenty-six thousand men drawing sword, besides inhabitants of Gibeah, who were counted as seven hundred chosen men.
From all this people were seven hundred chosen men restricted in right hand/left-handed; each could sling a stone at a hair and not miss.
Men of Israel, apart from Benjamin, were counted as four hundred thousand men drawing sword; every one was a man of war.
They arose and went up to Bethel and inquired of Divine. Children of Israel said, Who shall go up for us first to battle against children of Benjamin? YHWH said, Judah first.
Children of Israel arose in morning and camped against Gibeah.
Men of Israel went out to battle against Benjamin, and men of Israel arranged battle against them at Gibeah.
Children of Benjamin went out from Gibeah and destroyed to ground from Israel that day twenty-two thousand men.
The people, men of Israel, strengthened themselves and again arranged battle at place where they had arranged on first day.
Children of Israel went up and wept before YHWH until evening. They inquired of YHWH, saying, Shall I again draw near to battle against children of Benjamin my brother? YHWH said, Go up against him.
Children of Israel drew near to children of Benjamin on second day.
Benjamin went out against them from Gibeah on second day and destroyed to ground from children of Israel another eighteen thousand men; all these drew sword.
All children of Israel and all people went up and came to Bethel. They wept and sat there before YHWH, fasted that day until evening, and offered ascending offerings and peace offerings before YHWH.
Children of Israel inquired of YHWH, for ark of covenant of Divine was there in those days.

Phinehas son of Eleazar son of Aaron stood before it in those days, saying, Shall I again go out to battle against children of Benjamin my brother, or shall I cease? YHWH said, Go up, because tomorrow I will give him into your hand.
Israel set ambushes around Gibeah.
Children of Israel went up against children of Benjamin on third day and arranged against Gibeah as time after time.

Children of Benjamin went out to meet the people and were drawn away from city. They began to strike some of people dead as time after time on highways, one of which goes up to Bethel and one to Gibeah in field, about thirty men of Israel.
Children of Benjamin said, They are being struck down before us as at first. Children of Israel had said, Let us flee and draw them away from city to highways.
All men of Israel arose from their place and arranged at Baal-tamar, and ambush of Israel burst out from its place, from Maareh-geba.
Ten thousand chosen men from all Israel came opposite Gibeah, and battle was heavy. Benjamin did not know that disaster was touching them.
YHWH struck Benjamin before Israel, and children of Israel destroyed from Benjamin that day twenty-five thousand one hundred men; all these drew sword.
Children of Benjamin saw that they were struck down. Men of Israel had yielded ground to Benjamin because they trusted the ambush they had set against Gibeah.
The ambush hurried and rushed upon Gibeah. The ambush advanced and struck all city by edge of sword.
The appointed signal between men of Israel and the ambush was to make a great cloud of smoke rise from city.

Men of Israel turned in battle, and Benjamin began to strike dead among men of Israel, about thirty men, because they said, Surely, being struck down, they are struck down before us as in first battle.
The cloud began to rise from city, a pillar of smoke. Benjamin turned behind, and look, whole city went up toward heavens.
Men of Israel turned, and men of Benjamin were terrified, because they saw that disaster had touched them.
They turned before men of Israel toward way of wilderness, but battle overtook them, and those who came from cities were destroying them in their midst.
They surrounded Benjamin, pursued them, and trod them down at their resting place as far as opposite Gibeah toward rising of sun.
From Benjamin fell eighteen thousand men; all these were men of strength.
They turned and fled toward wilderness to rock of Rimmon. Israel gleaned from them on highways five thousand men, clung after them as far as Gidom, and struck from them two thousand men.
All who fell from Benjamin that day were twenty-five thousand men drawing sword; all these were men of strength.
Six hundred men turned and fled toward wilderness to rock of Rimmon, and they dwelt at rock of Rimmon four months.
Men of Israel returned to children of Benjamin and struck them by edge of sword, from inhabited city to animal to all that was found. Also all cities that were found they sent into fire.

translation_notes: The national assembly forms around the Levite's circulated body parts, but the murdered woman remains unnamed and cannot testify. His account omits that he himself seized and surrendered her and later dismembered her body. Public testimony must be evaluated for self-exoneration and missing survivor voice.

The demand in verse 13 targets perpetrators in Gibeah, but Benjamin's refusal converts criminal accountability into tribal solidarity. Protecting abusers because they belong to one's own group deepens harm; it does not make collective extermination just.
Calling violent offenders 'sons of beliyyaal' is an ancient idiom for lawlessness, not a claim that any people are ontologically worthless or beyond human dignity.

The large troop and casualty figures may reflect epic or schematic historiography and require later textual and historical audit. Whatever their scale, the narrative presents catastrophic Israelite-on-Israelite bloodshed.
The seven hundred left-handed or right-hand-restricted slingers are praised for skill. Bodily difference is not defect, shame, or evidence of moral danger.

Israel repeatedly inquires of YHWH and receives battle directions, yet suffers enormous losses and ultimately commits near-extermination. Oracle language inside this tragic narrative may not be abstracted into permission for religious war, certainty about violence, or claims that victory proves righteousness.
The presence of Phinehas and the ark creates chronological and compositional questions within Judges. Later audit should not flatten these traditions into a simple modern war chronology.
The third battle uses deliberate retreat, ambush, fire, and a smoke signal. Tactical description is not moral endorsement or instruction for vigilantism.

Verse 35 attributes Benjamin's defeat to YHWH, while surrounding verses explain the ambush. Theological causation and human military action coexist in the narrative; neither erases responsibility for what follows.
The pursuit continues after the battlefield collapse and kills fleeing Benjaminites. Only six hundred reach Rimmon. Refusal of surrender or restraint turns punishment into tribal destruction.
Verse 48 extends violence from combatants to settlements, inhabitants, animals, property, and fire. This is collective punishment and near-genocide, not justice for the woman harmed at Gibeah.

The chapter's stated goal of removing evil ends by multiplying death and destroying communities. Justice that abandons proportionality, survivors, civilians, and future life becomes another form of the outrage it claims to answer.
safety_note: Maximum anti-tribal-violence and anti-genocide safeguards: Do not use Divine inquiry, majority unity, perpetrator-protection, military loss, or victory to sanctify collective punishment. Benjaminite civilians, fugitives, animals, homes, and future generations are not legitimate substitutes for accountable perpetrators. Do not glorify civil war, ambush, burning cities, extermination, or retaliation as survivor justice. The murdered woman's body must not be used again to recruit hatred against an ethnicity, tribe, religion, gender, sexuality, or modern population.
